title: धन्वन्तरि अष्टकम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री धन्वन्तरि अष्टकम् ॥

॥ ध्यानम् ॥
शंखं चक्रं जलौकां च दधतं अमृतघटम्।
चतुर्भुजं पीतवासं धन्वन्तरिमहं भजे॥

॥ स्तोत्रम् ॥
नमामि धन्वन्तरिमादिदेवं सुरासुरैर्वन्दितपादपद्मम्।
लोके जराव्याधिविनाशदक्षं दातारमीशं विविधौषधीनाम्॥ १॥

क्षीरोदमथनोद्भूतं शंखचक्रादिधारिणम्।
आयुर्वेदप्रवक्तारं धन्वन्तरिमहं भजे॥ २॥

मृत्युव्याधिजराहर्तारं अमृतकलशधारिणम्।
विष्णोरंशं महात्मानं धन्वन्तरिमहं भजे॥ ३॥

अष्टाङ्गायुर्वेदकर्तारं शरीरशास्त्रप्रवर्तकम्।
त्रिदोषज्ञं महावैद्यं धन्वन्तरिमहं भजे॥ ४॥

निदानज्ञं विकारघ्नं चिकित्साशास्त्रपारगम्।
सर्वरोगहरं शान्तं धन्वन्तरिमहं भजे॥ ५॥

महौषधिप्रदातारं रसायनविशारदम्।
शल्यतन्त्रं महाज्ञानं धन्वन्तरिमहं भजे॥ ६॥

दीर्घायुषं प्रदातारं सर्वामयनिवारकम्।
शरीरसौख्यकर्तारं धन्वन्तरिमहं भजे॥ ७॥

भेषजं भिषजां वैद्यं भवरोगहरं हरिम्।
सर्वामृतमयं देवं धन्वन्तरिमहं भजे॥ ८॥

॥ फलश्रुति ॥
धन्वन्तर्यष्टकं यो हि पठेत् प्रातः समाहितः।
सर्वरोगविनिर्मुक्तो दीर्घायुर्जायते नरः॥

वैद्यकर्मसु सिद्धिः स्यात् चिकित्सायां विशेषतः।
धन्वन्तरिप्रसादेन आरोग्यं लभते ध्रुवम्॥

॥ इति श्री धन्वन्तरि अष्टकं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Dhanvantari Ashtakam ||

|| Dhyanam ||
Shankham Chakram Jalaukam Cha Dadhatam Amrita-ghatam.
Chatur-bhujam Pita-vasam Dhanvantarim-aham Bhaje.

|| Stotram ||
Namami Dhanvantarim-adi-devam Sura-asurair-vandita-pada-padmam.
Loke Jara-vyadhi-vinasha-daksham Dataram-isham Vividha-aushadhinam. || 1 ||

Kshiroda-mathana-udbhutam Shankha-chakra-adi-dharinam.
Ayurveda-pravaktaram Dhanvantarim-aham Bhaje. || 2 ||

Mrityu-vyadhi-jara-hartaram Amrita-kalasha-dharinam.
Vishnor-amsham Mahatmanam Dhanvantarim-aham Bhaje. || 3 ||

Ashtanga-ayurveda-kartaram Sharira-shastra-pravartakam.
Tri-dosha-jnam Maha-vaidyam Dhanvantarim-aham Bhaje. || 4 ||

Nidana-jnam Vikara-ghnam Chikitsa-shastra-paragam.
Sarva-roga-haram Shantam Dhanvantarim-aham Bhaje. || 5 ||

Maha-aushadhi-pradataram Rasayana-visharadam.
Shalya-tantram Maha-jnanam Dhanvantarim-aham Bhaje. || 6 ||

Dirgha-ayusham Pradataram Sarva-amaya-nivarakam.
Sharira-saukhya-kartaram Dhanvantarim-aham Bhaje. || 7 ||

Bheshajam Bhishajam Vaidyam Bhava-roga-haram Harim.
Sarva-amritamayam Devam Dhanvantarim-aham Bhaje. || 8 ||

|| Phalashruti ||
Dhanvantary-ashtakam Yo Hi Pathet Pratah Samahitah.
Sarva-roga-vinirmukto Dirgha-ayur-jayate Narah.

Vaidya-karmasu Siddhih Syat Chikitsayam Visheshatah.
Dhanvantari-prasadena Arogyam Labhate Dhruvam.

|| Iti Shri Dhanvantari Ashtakam Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
शंख, चक्र, जलौका (जोंक) और अमृत घट धारण किये, पीतवस्त्रधारी, चतुर्भुज धन्वन्तरि का भजन करता हूँ।

आदिदेव धन्वन्तरि को नमन जिनके चरण कमलों की सुर-असुर वन्दना करते हैं, जो जरा-व्याधि विनाश में दक्ष हैं, विविध औषधियों के दाता ईश्वर हैं।

क्षीरसागर मन्थन से उत्पन्न, शंख-चक्रादि धारी, आयुर्वेद के प्रवक्ता। मृत्यु-व्याधि-जरा के हर्ता, अमृतकलश धारी, विष्णु के अंश, महात्मा।

अष्टांग आयुर्वेद के कर्ता, शरीरशास्त्र के प्रवर्तक, त्रिदोषज्ञ, महावैद्य। निदानज्ञ, विकारघ्न, चिकित्साशास्त्रपारगत, सर्वरोगहर।

जो प्रातःकाल समाहित होकर इस अष्टक का पाठ करता है, वह सर्वरोग से मुक्त होकर दीर्घायु होता है। वैद्यकर्म में सिद्धि मिलती है, धन्वन्तरि के प्रसाद से निश्चित आरोग्य प्राप्त होता है।
benefits: Invokes freedom from all diseases and chronic ailments
Invokes the blessing of long life and vitality
Especially powerful for medical practitioners seeking success
Associated in tradition with through the divine grace of lord dhanvantari
Balances tridosha and promotes holistic health
Traditionally invoked for protection from epidemics and life-threatening diseases
description: Dhanvantari Ashtakam — धन्वन्तरि अष्टकम् — is an eight-verse devotional hymn offered to Lord Dhanvantari. This sacred composition is sourced from Bhagavata Purana and continues to inspire devotees worldwide. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to bestow long life and vitality as well as grant freedom from all diseases and chronic ailments. Additionally, it is known to heal through the divine grace of Lord Dhanvantari. For best results, devotees chant this on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Thursday (Guruvar) and during Dhanteras and Diwali. At 8 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 8 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Dhanvantari Ashtakam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Dhanvantari Ashtakam and what does it praise?
seoFaqs.answer: The Dhanvantari Ashtakam (धन्वन्तरि अष्टकम्) is an eight-verse devotional hymn in praise of Lord Dhanvantari — the divine physician (deva-vaidya) of the gods, the Adi-deva of Ayurveda, and an amsha-avatar of Lord Vishnu who emerged from the Kshira Sagara during the Samudra Manthana bearing the kalasha of amrita (the nectar of immortality). The hymn opens with a dhyana shloka that fixes the divine form: holding the shankha (conch), chakra (discus), jalauka (the medicinal leech) and amrita-ghata (nectar pot), four-armed, clad in golden silks. Each subsequent verse ends Dhanvantarim-aham bhaje — 'I worship Dhanvantari' — celebrating him as the destroyer of jara-vyadhi (decay and disease), the founder of Ashtanga Ayurveda, the master of tridosha-vidya, the giver of dirgha-ayus (long life), and the supreme bheshaja-bhishaj — the medicine of all medicines. (Sources: Bhagavata Purana 8.8; Ayurveda samhita tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: Who is Lord Dhanvantari and where does he originate?
seoFaqs.answer: Lord Dhanvantari is celebrated in Srimad Bhagavata Purana (Skanda 8, Adhyaya 8) as the divine physician who arose from the churning of the cosmic ocean (Samudra Manthana). When the devas and asuras churned the Kshira Sagara using Mount Mandara as the rod and Vasuki the serpent as the rope, fourteen ratnas emerged in sequence; Dhanvantari was the thirteenth, rising from the waves on the fourteenth day of Krishna Paksha in Kartik (Dhanteras), bearing the golden kalasha of amrita. He is worshipped as the Adi-Vaidya (original physician), the propounder of Ayurveda, and patron deity of all healers. The Charaka Samhita and Sushruta Samhita both venerate him as the rishi-source of the eight branches of Ayurveda — particularly Shalya Tantra (surgery). He is considered the eighteenth amsha-avatar of Lord Vishnu. (Sources: Bhagavata Purana 8.8.32-35; Sushruta Samhita Sutrasthana.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the iconography of Lord Dhanvantari described in this hymn?
seoFaqs.answer: The dhyana of the Dhanvantari Ashtakam fixes the four-armed form of the deva-vaidya with extraordinary precision. In his hands he bears: the shankha (conch — purifying sound), the chakra (discus — protective dharma), the jalauka (the medicinal leech — surgical instrument symbolising the entire field of Ayurvedic therapy), and the amrita-ghata (the golden pot of nectar). His form is chaturbhuja (four-armed), pita-vasa (clad in yellow silks), and in some verses described as nilotpala-dala-shyama (dark-blue like the lotus petal), padma-alankrita-bhushana (adorned with lotus ornaments), and seated upon a lotus throne. This four-armed iconography is enshrined in the famous Dhanvantari temples at Thottuva (Kerala), Nelluvai, Maruthorvattom, and the Dhanvantari shrine at Sringeri Sharada Peetha. (Sources: Dhanvantari Ashtakam dhyana shloka; Ayurveda iconographic tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is Dhanvantari connected to Ayurveda and Ashtanga medicine?
seoFaqs.answer: Verse 4 — Ashtanga-ayurveda-kartaram sharira-shastra-pravartakam, tri-dosha-jnam maha-vaidyam Dhanvantarim-aham bhaje — explicitly praises Lord Dhanvantari as the founder (karta) of Ashtanga Ayurveda and the propounder (pravartaka) of sharira-shastra (the science of the body). Ashtanga Ayurveda comprises eight branches: Kayachikitsa (internal medicine), Shalya Tantra (surgery), Shalakya Tantra (ENT and ophthalmology), Kaumarabhritya (paediatrics), Agada Tantra (toxicology), Bhuta Vidya (psychiatry), Rasayana (rejuvenation), and Vajikarana (virilification). Tridosha-jna refers to his mastery of the doctrine of vata, pitta and kapha — the three humours whose balance determines health. The Sushruta Samhita opens with King Divodasa Dhanvantari teaching surgery to Sushruta and other rishis at Kashi — Sushruta therefore being the human guru-parampara of Dhanvantari's surgical science. (Sources: Sushruta Samhita 1.1; Charaka Samhita; Ayurveda tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: When and how should the Dhanvantari Ashtakam be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The Dhanvantari Ashtakam is most powerfully recited on Dhanvantari Jayanti (Dhanteras — Kartik Krishna Trayodashi, two days before Diwali, marking the day Dhanvantari emerged from the ocean); on every Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Thursday (Guruvar); during Diwali week; on every Pradosh; and continuously by patients seeking arogya, by Ayurveda physicians and students before consultation or surgery, and by families during epidemics or chronic illness. The recitation should be done at Brahma muhurta after bath, before an image of Dhanvantari with a tulsi-mala, lighting a ghee lamp and offering tulsi, kumkum and flowers. Doctors traditionally recite it 11 or 21 times before beginning their day. The phalashruti promises that the morning reciter becomes sarva-roga-vinirmukto and dirgha-ayur. (Sources: Dhanvantari upasana paddhati; Ayurveda traditions.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the benefits of reciting the Dhanvantari Ashtakam?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti of the Dhanvantari Ashtakam itself promises: sarva-roga-vinirmukto dirgha-ayur-jayate narah — the early-morning reciter is freed from all diseases and becomes long-lived; vaidya-karmasu siddhih syat chikitsayam visheshatah — success in all healing work and especially in clinical treatment; Dhanvantari-prasadena arogyam labhate dhruvam — by the grace of Dhanvantari, perfect health is attained without doubt. Traditional Ayurveda acharyas additionally extol: balancing of vata-pitta-kapha doshas; rejuvenation of saptadhatu (the seven body tissues); freedom from chronic and epidemic disease; protection from akala-mrityu (untimely death); success of all medical treatment, surgery and rasayana; relief from old-age decline; and ultimately moksha — for the bhava-roga (the disease of samsara) is also cured by the supreme Vaidya. The Ashtakam is therefore the foremost arogya-stotra of Sanatana Dharma. (Sources: Dhanvantari Ashtakam Phalashruti.)
